exampleSentence: Compulsory education typically spans ages 5 to 16.

definition: Money borrowed to fund education expenses
exampleSentence: Student loan debt affects graduates' financial decisions for decades.

definition: Learning through physical activity and movement
exampleSentence: Kinesthetic learning involves hands-on experiments and activities.

definition: Publication containing scholarly research articles
exampleSentence: Academic journals undergo rigorous peer review before publication.

definition: Learning throughout adult life beyond formal schooling
exampleSentence: Continuing education allows professionals to update their skills.

definition: Scoring guide for evaluating student work
exampleSentence: The grading rubric clarifies expectations for essay assignments.

definition: Undergraduate academic degree
exampleSentence: A bachelor degree typically requires three to four years of study.

definition: Adjustments made to support student learning
exampleSentence: Exam accommodations include extra time for students with disabilities.

definition: Annual period of educational instruction
exampleSentence: The academic year is divided into two semesters.

definition: Person who learns best through listening
exampleSentence: Auditory learners benefit from lectures and discussion groups.

definition: Instruction in two languages
exampleSentence: Bilingual education programs help preserve native languages.

definition: Repeated aggressive behavior toward a student
exampleSentence: Anti-bullying policies create safer school environments.

definition: Grounds and buildings of a university
exampleSentence: The campus includes libraries, dormitories and lecture halls.

definition: Final culminating academic project
exampleSentence: The capstone project demonstrates mastery of program learning outcomes.

definition: Number of students in a class
exampleSentence: Smaller class sizes improve student learning outcomes.

definition: Education of males and females together
exampleSentence: Most modern schools practice coeducation rather than single-sex education.

definition: Mental effort used in working memory
exampleSentence: Teachers should avoid excessive cognitive load when presenting new material.

definition: Objective analysis and evaluation of issues
exampleSentence: Critical thinking skills are essential for academic success.

definition: Structured course of study leading to a qualification
exampleSentence: The degree program includes both core and elective courses.

definition: Education delivered remotely, often online
exampleSentence: Distance learning expanded significantly during the pandemic.

definition: Percentage of students leaving school before completion
exampleSentence: High dropout rates are linked to socioeconomic disadvantage.

definition: Formal education for young children before primary school
exampleSentence: Early childhood education provides foundation for later learning.

definition: Learning conducted via electronic media
exampleSentence: E-learning platforms offer flexible self-paced courses.

definition: Process of registering for educational courses
exampleSentence: University enrollment has increased globally over the past decade.

definition: Short written piece on a particular subject
exampleSentence: The essay requires critical analysis of primary sources.

definition: Teaching and research staff of a university
exampleSentence: The faculty includes professors from diverse academic backgrounds.

definition: Person who has completed a degree or diploma
exampleSentence: Graduates from this program have excellent employment prospects.

definition: Selective secondary school focusing on academic subjects
exampleSentence: Grammar schools admit students based on entrance examination scores.

definition: Financial aid that does not require repayment
exampleSentence: The research grant funded a three-year study on climate adaptation.

definition: Educating children at home rather than in schools
exampleSentence: Home schooling allows customized learning approaches.

definition: Inability to read or write
exampleSentence: Illiteracy limits economic opportunities and social mobility.

definition: Education accessible to all students regardless of needs
exampleSentence: Inclusive education integrates students with disabilities into mainstream classrooms.

definition: Creations of the mind protected by law
exampleSentence: Universities protect intellectual property developed through research.

definition: Combining multiple academic disciplines
exampleSentence: Interdisciplinary studies address complex real-world problems.

definition: Preschool educational program for young children
exampleSentence: Kindergarten prepares children for formal schooling.

definition: Large room for teaching university courses
exampleSentence: The lecture hall seats 300 students for introductory courses.

definition: Standard education system for most students
exampleSentence: Mainstream education may not suit all learning styles.

definition: Experienced advisor who guides less experienced person
exampleSentence: A good mentor provides guidance for academic and career development.

definition: Question format with several answer options
exampleSentence: Multiple-choice exams efficiently test broad knowledge.

definition: Standardized educational content set by government
exampleSentence: The National Curriculum establishes learning standards for all schools.

definition: Educational course delivered over the internet
exampleSentence: Online courses offer flexibility for working professionals.

definition: University with open admission and distance learning
exampleSentence: The Open University provides accessible higher education worldwide.

definition: Not attending formal education
exampleSentence: Out-of-school children face increased poverty risk.

definition: Organization involving parents and teachers in school life
exampleSentence: The parent-teacher association raises funds for school activities.

definition: Highest university research degree
exampleSentence: A PhD requires original research contributing new knowledge.

definition: Using others' work without attribution
exampleSentence: Plagiarism can result in academic expulsion.

definition: Relating to study beyond bachelor degree level
exampleSentence: Postgraduate programs include master and doctoral degrees.

definition: First stage of formal schooling for young children
exampleSentence: Primary education develops foundational literacy and numeracy skills.

definition: Required prior condition or course
exampleSentence: Calculus is a prerequisite for advanced physics courses.

definition: Ongoing learning to improve professional skills
exampleSentence: Professional development workshops help teachers update their methods.

definition: High degree of competence in a skill
exampleSentence: Language proficiency is assessed through standardized tests.

definition: Ability to understand written text
exampleSentence: Reading comprehension tests evaluate understanding of complex passages.

definition: Additional instruction for students needing extra help
exampleSentence: Remedial classes help struggling students meet grade-level standards.

definition: Document summarizing student academic performance
exampleSentence: Report cards are issued at the end of each semester.

definition: Plan for a proposed research project
exampleSentence: The research proposal must outline methodology and expected outcomes.

definition: Retaking an examination after failing
exampleSentence: Students may take a resit if they fail the initial exam.

definition: Geographic area with its own school administration
exampleSentence: School districts allocate funding across local schools.

definition: Student who leaves school before completion
exampleSentence: School dropout rates are higher in disadvantaged communities.

definition: Education between primary and higher education
exampleSentence: Secondary education prepares students for university or vocational training.

definition: Small group discussion-based academic session
exampleSentence: Weekly seminars encourage in-depth discussion of research papers.

definition: Specially designed instruction for students with disabilities
exampleSentence: Special education provides individualized learning plans.

definition: Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics education
exampleSentence: STEM education is critical for technological workforce development.

definition: Program allowing study at a foreign institution
exampleSentence: Student exchange programs foster cross-cultural understanding.

definition: Pursuing education in a foreign country
exampleSentence: Studying abroad enhances language skills and global perspective.

definition: Final evaluation at end of a learning period
exampleSentence: Summative assessment measures overall achievement at course completion.

definition: Person who supports teachers in classroom instruction
exampleSentence: The teaching assistant helps students with individual questions.

definition: Permanent employment status for academics
exampleSentence: Academic tenure protects professors' freedom to pursue controversial research.

definition: Numerical result of an assessment
exampleSentence: Test scores are used for university admission decisions.

definition: Long research paper for a university degree
exampleSentence: The master's thesis requires original research of 15,000 words.

definition: Structured course developing specific skills
exampleSentence: The training program prepares employees for new responsibilities.

definition: Money paid for educational instruction
exampleSentence: Rising tuition fees make higher education less accessible.

definition: Small teaching session with individual attention
exampleSentence: Tutorials provide personalized academic guidance.

definition: Student pursuing bachelor degree
exampleSentence: Undergraduate students choose their major in the second year.

definition: Visual material supporting learning or presentation
exampleSentence: Visual aids like diagrams improve student understanding of complex concepts.

definition: School teaching practical trade skills
exampleSentence: Vocational schools offer certificates in fields like plumbing and electrical work.

definition: Learning through practical workplace experience
exampleSentence: Apprenticeships combine work-based learning with classroom instruction.

definition: Official recognition of educational quality
exampleSentence: University accreditation ensures programs meet academic standards.

definition: Process of being accepted into an institution
exampleSentence: University admission requires satisfactory test scores and grades.

definition: Former graduates of an institution
exampleSentence: University alumni networks provide career connections for graduates.

definition: Training combining work and study for a trade
exampleSentence: Apprenticeships offer earn-while-you-learn career pathways.

definition: Act of being present at educational sessions
exampleSentence: Regular attendance is linked to better academic performance.

definition: Financial grant for education based on need
exampleSentence: The bursary covers textbooks and materials for low-income students.

definition: Strategies for maintaining orderly learning environment
exampleSentence: Effective classroom management maximizes instructional time.

definition: General increase in prices reducing purchasing power
exampleSentence: High inflation erodes household savings and living standards.

definition: Increasing interconnection of economies worldwide
exampleSentence: Globalization has expanded international trade and cultural exchange.

definition: Activity of starting and running businesses
exampleSentence: Entrepreneurship drives innovation and job creation.

definition: Government decisions on taxation and spending
exampleSentence: Fiscal policy influences economic growth through tax and spending adjustments.

definition: Activity of buying and selling goods and services
exampleSentence: E-commerce has transformed global commerce patterns.

definition: Imports exceeding exports in value
exampleSentence: A persistent trade deficit can weaken a country's currency.

definition: Purchase of assets with potential to generate returns
exampleSentence: Foreign direct investment boosts developing economies.

definition: Exclusive control of a market by one seller
exampleSentence: The monopoly prevented competition and kept prices high.

definition: Removal of government regulations on industry
exampleSentence: Deregulation of the airline industry lowered ticket prices.

definition: Government financial support to reduce costs
exampleSentence: Agricultural subsidies help farmers maintain stable incomes.

definition: Tax on imported or exported goods
exampleSentence: Import tariffs protect domestic industries from foreign competition.

definition: Economic system driven by supply and demand
exampleSentence: Market economies rely on price signals to allocate resources.

definition: Period of temporary economic decline
exampleSentence: The recession led to widespread job losses across the manufacturing sector.

definition: Total value of goods and services produced in a country
exampleSentence: GDP per capita is a common measure of economic prosperity.

definition: Percentage charged on borrowed money
exampleSentence: Central banks adjust interest rates to control inflation.

definition: International trade without restrictions or tariffs
exampleSentence: Free trade agreements reduce barriers between partner countries.

definition: Measure of optimism about economic conditions
exampleSentence: Consumer confidence affects spending and economic growth.

definition: Government spending exceeding revenue
exampleSentence: The government aims to reduce the budget deficit through spending cuts.

definition: Tax on company profits
exampleSentence: Lower corporate tax rates attract multinational companies.

definition: Funding for early-stage high-growth businesses
exampleSentence: Venture capital fuels innovation in the technology sector.

definition: Market where shares of companies are traded
exampleSentence: Stock market volatility increased during the economic crisis.

definition: Contracting work to external companies
exampleSentence: Outsourcing manufacturing reduces labor costs for corporations.

definition: Total demand for goods and services in an economy
exampleSentence: Falling aggregate demand triggered the economic downturn.

definition: Record of all transactions between a country and the rest of the world
exampleSentence: The balance of payments includes trade and financial flows.

definition: Obstacles preventing new competitors from entering a market
exampleSentence: High capital requirements are barriers to entry in the airline industry.

definition: Market where debt securities are traded
exampleSentence: The bond market provides governments with long-term funding.

definition: Economic cycle of rapid growth followed by contraction
exampleSentence: The housing market experienced a classic boom and bust cycle.

definition: Profit from selling an asset
exampleSentence: Investors pay tax on capital gains from property sales.

definition: Group of producers colluding to control prices
exampleSentence: The oil cartel agreed to reduce production to raise prices.

definition: National institution managing currency and monetary policy
exampleSentence: The central bank raised interest rates to combat inflation.

definition: Asset pledged as security for a loan
exampleSentence: The bank required property as collateral for the business loan.

definition: Measure of average price changes over time
exampleSentence: The consumer price index rose 3% indicating moderate inflation.

definition: Money needed to maintain a basic standard of living
exampleSentence: The cost of living in major cities has risen dramatically.

definition: Sudden reduction in availability of loans
exampleSentence: The credit crunch made it difficult for small businesses to borrow.

definition: Decrease in currency value relative to others
exampleSentence: Currency depreciation makes exports cheaper but imports more expensive.

definition: Situation where a country cannot repay its debts
exampleSentence: The debt crisis forced the government to seek international bailout.

definition: Failure to repay a loan
exampleSentence: The company defaulted on its bond payments.

definition: Sustained decrease in general price levels
exampleSentence: Deflation can discourage spending as consumers wait for lower prices.

definition: Official lowering of currency value
exampleSentence: Currency devaluation boosted export competitiveness.

definition: Income remaining after taxes and essential expenses
exampleSentence: Rising disposable income drives consumer spending growth.

definition: Share of profits paid to shareholders
exampleSentence: The company increased its dividend after a profitable year.

definition: Cost advantages from larger production volumes
exampleSentence: Large manufacturers benefit from economies of scale.

definition: Official ban on trade with a country
exampleSentence: The trade embargo severely damaged the country's economy.

definition: Value of shares or ownership in a company
exampleSentence: The startup raised equity funding from venture capitalists.

definition: Group of EU countries using the euro as currency
exampleSentence: The eurozone shares a single monetary policy.

definition: Value of one currency in terms of another
exampleSentence: Fluctuating exchange rates affect international trade profits.

definition: Tax on specific goods like alcohol and fuel
exampleSentence: The government raised excise duty on tobacco products.

definition: Government payment to encourage exports
exampleSentence: Export subsidies help domestic producers compete internationally.

definition: Country's debt owed to foreign creditors
exampleSentence: High external debt burdens limit economic development.

definition: Government spending increase to boost economic activity
exampleSentence: The fiscal stimulus package included infrastructure projects.

definition: Currency value pegged to another currency or commodity
exampleSentence: China maintained a fixed exchange rate against the dollar for years.

definition: Investment from abroad controlling business operations
exampleSentence: Foreign direct investment creates jobs in host countries.

definition: License to operate using an established brand
exampleSentence: The franchise expanded rapidly through local operators.

definition: Market with minimal government intervention
exampleSentence: Free market advocates argue for reduced regulation.

definition: Labor market with short-term flexible jobs
exampleSentence: The gig economy offers flexibility but limited job security.

definition: Debt security issued by a government
exampleSentence: Government bonds are considered low-risk investments.

definition: Revenue minus cost of goods sold
exampleSentence: Gross profit margin indicates production efficiency.

definition: Investment fund using advanced strategies
exampleSentence: Hedge funds use leverage to amplify returns.

definition: Skills and knowledge contributing to economic productivity
exampleSentence: Investment in human capital through education drives growth.

definition: Limit on quantity of imports allowed
exampleSentence: Import quotas protect domestic producers from foreign competition.

definition: Uneven distribution of income across a population
exampleSentence: Income inequality has increased in many developed nations.

definition: Percentage increase in prices over time
exampleSentence: Central banks target an inflation rate around 2 percent.

definition: Inability to pay debts when due
exampleSentence: The company filed for insolvency after declining sales.

definition: Cost of borrowing money or return on savings
exampleSentence: Savings accounts earn interest on deposited funds.

definition: Business partnership between two or more parties
exampleSentence: The joint venture combined expertise from both companies.

definition: Economic theory emphasizing government spending to manage demand
exampleSentence: Keynesian economics advocates stimulus during recessions.

definition: Supply and demand for workers
exampleSentence: The labour market tightened as unemployment fell.

definition: Use of borrowed funds for investment
exampleSentence: High leverage amplifies both gains and losses.

definition: Legal financial obligation or debt
exampleSentence: The company's liabilities exceeded its assets.

definition: Ease of converting assets to cash
exampleSentence: The bank maintains sufficient liquidity to meet withdrawals.

definition: Relating to the economy as a whole
exampleSentence: Macroeconomic indicators include GDP and unemployment rates.

definition: Cost of producing one additional unit
exampleSentence: Marginal cost decreases as production scales up.

definition: Percentage of total sales in a market
exampleSentence: The company gained market share through aggressive pricing.

definition: Combination of two companies into one
exampleSentence: The merger created the largest telecommunications company in the region.

definition: Small loans to low-income borrowers
exampleSentence: Microfinance helps entrepreneurs in developing countries.

definition: Lowest legal hourly pay rate
exampleSentence: The government increased the minimum wage to reduce poverty.

definition: Central bank actions controlling money supply
exampleSentence: Monetary policy adjustments influence borrowing and spending.

definition: Company operating in multiple countries
exampleSentence: Multinational corporations benefit from global supply chains.

definition: Total amount owed by a government
exampleSentence: The national debt grew substantially during the economic crisis.

definition: Property worth less than the mortgage owed
exampleSentence: Falling house prices left many homeowners in negative equity.

definition: Profit after all expenses and taxes
exampleSentence: Net profit margin reflects overall business profitability.

definition: Market dominated by a few large sellers
exampleSentence: The telecommunications market is an oligopoly in many countries.

definition: Value of the best alternative foregone
exampleSentence: Choosing one investment involves opportunity costs of alternatives.

definition: Ongoing business expenses not directly tied to production
exampleSentence: Reducing overhead improves company profitability.

definition: Exclusive right to an invention
exampleSentence: The company holds a patent for its innovative battery technology.

definition: Collection of investments held by an individual
exampleSentence: A diverse portfolio reduces investment risk.

definition: Transfer of public services to private ownership
exampleSentence: Privatization of utilities aimed to improve efficiency.

definition: Efficiency of output per unit of input
exampleSentence: Higher productivity leads to economic growth without inflation.

definition: Percentage of revenue retained as profit
exampleSentence: Thin profit margins require high sales volumes.

definition: Policy of shielding domestic industry from imports
exampleSentence: Protectionism raises consumer prices but protects local jobs.

definition: Central bank creating money to stimulate economy
exampleSentence: Quantitative easing increased money supply after the financial crisis.

definition: Limited quantity of imports or exports allowed
exampleSentence: Import quotas restrict foreign competition in sensitive industries.

definition: Property consisting of land and buildings
exampleSentence: Real estate is considered a stable long-term investment.

definition: Job loss because position is no longer needed
exampleSentence: The factory closure led to mass redundancies.

definition: Tax taking larger percentage from lower incomes
exampleSentence: Sales tax is regressive as it consumes more of low incomes.

definition: Profit relative to the amount invested
exampleSentence: The marketing campaign delivered a strong return on investment.

definition: Network involved in producing and delivering products
exampleSentence: Global supply chain disruptions affected product availability.

definition: Illegal avoidance of tax payment
exampleSentence: Tax evasion undermines public finances and services.

definition: How wealth is spread across a population
exampleSentence: Unequal wealth distribution creates social tensions.

definition: Funds available for day-to-day business operations
exampleSentence: Sufficient working capital ensures smooth business operations.
